question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with an adminKey set to a zero-length byte array, causing transactionBody.getAdminKey().toByteArray() to return an empty array rather than null, and during a network partition one node stores an empty adminKey (treating the topic as having an explicit empty key) while another stores null (treating it as having no key), diverging topic mutability state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where entity.setCreatedTimestamp(consensusTimestamp) is set in AbstractEntityCrudTransactionHandler.updateEntity() but the subsequent doUpdateEntity() call fails on one partition, causing the entity to have a createdTimestamp but no corresponding topic row, permanently corrupting the entity-topic relationship?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with an autoRenewAccount that is a valid account on one partition but has been deleted on another partition (due to partition-induced state divergence), causing entity.setAutoRenewAccountId() to store a deleted account ID as the auto-renew account, enabling a denial-of-service where the topic cannot be auto-renewed after partition healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a submitKey containing a ThresholdKey with threshold value of 0, causing transactionBody.getSubmitKey().toByteArray() to store a key that allows any message submission, and during a network partition one node stores this permissive key while another rejects it, creating divergent topic message submission access control?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the transaction is processed by one importer node but the corresponding record file is not yet available to the other node, causing the second node to later process the same transaction with a different RecordItem state (e.g., different sidecarRecords), resulting in divergent topic metadata?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a custom fee referencing a token ID that is being created in the same batch, such that during a network partition the token creation is processed on one node but not the other before updateCustomFee() runs, causing EntityId.of(fixedFee.getDenominatingTokenId()) to resolve to different values across partitions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a feeExemptKeyList containing duplicate keys, causing FeeExemptKeyList.newBuilder().addAllKeys(transactionBody.getFeeExemptKeyListList()).build().toByteArray() to serialize a list with duplicates, and during a network partition one node deduplicates the list while another does not, creating divergent fee exemption state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the AbstractEntityCrudTransactionHandler sets entity.setDeleted(false) on one partition but the entity already exists as deleted on the other partition (due to a prior delete that only reached one partition), causing the create to resurrect a deleted entity on one node but not the other?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a memo containing null bytes or special Unicode characters, causing entity.setMemo(transactionBody.getMemo()) to store a string that is encoded differently on two partitioned nodes (e.g., due to different JVM charset configurations), resulting in divergent memo state after partition healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a consensusTimestamp of 0 (epoch), causing Range.atLeast(0) to create a range starting at the minimum timestamp, and during a network partition this zero timestamp causes incorrect ordering of topic history entries on one node, corrupting the topic's temporal state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the consensusUpdateTopicTransactionHandler.updateCustomFee() call succeeds on one partition but throws an ImporterException on the other (due to a transient database error), causing the topic to have custom fees on one partition but not the other, permanently diverging fee state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with an autoRenewPeriod of Long.MAX_VALUE seconds, causing entity.setAutoRenewPeriod(Long.MAX_VALUE) to store an overflow value, and during a network partition one node stores this value while another rejects it as invalid, creating divergent auto-renewal period state that causes the topic to never expire on one partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a feeScheduleKey that is identical to the adminKey, and during a network partition one node processes the transaction and stores both keys while the other node only stores the adminKey (due to a partial write failure), creating divergent key state where the fee schedule is unprotected on one partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the entity.setTimestampLower(consensusTimestamp) call in AbstractEntityCrudTransactionHandler sets a timestamp that is in the future relative to one partition's clock, causing that partition to reject the entity write while the other accepts it, resulting in the topic existing on only one partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a custom fee list where one fee has a zero amount, causing FixedFee.builder().amount(0) to store a zero-amount fee, and during a network partition one node stores this zero-amount fee while another filters it out as invalid, creating divergent custom fee state that enables fee bypass on the node that filtered it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a submitKey containing a KeyList with more keys than the maximum allowed, causing transactionBody.getSubmitKey().toByteArray() to produce an oversized byte array, and during a network partition one node stores the oversized key while another truncates it, creating divergent submit key state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the RecordItem.isSuccessful() check in AbstractEntityCrudTransactionHandler evaluates to true on one partition (because the receipt status is SUCCESS) but the transaction was actually rolled back on the consensus layer due to the partition, causing a phantom topic entity to be created on one mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a custom fee collector account that is the same as the topic creator's account, and during a network partition the recordItem.addEntityId(collector) call on one partition adds the creator's entity ID to the entity transaction tracking while the other partition does not, causing divergent entity transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with an adminKey that is a valid key on one partition but has been revoked/rotated on another partition (due to partition-induced key rotation divergence), causing transactionBody.getAdminKey().toByteArray() to store a stale key on one partition, enabling unauthorized topic administration after partition healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the Topic.builder().createdTimestamp(consensusTimestamp) is set on one partition but the entityListener.onTopic() call fails on the other partition, causing the topic to have a createdTimestamp in the entity table but no corresponding row in the topic table on one partition?
